title: codariocalyx motorius var. roylei
intro: Codariocalyx motorius var. roylei, commonly known as the climbing calamite or snake plant of the forest, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical forests in India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Codariocalyx motorius var. roylei include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
